methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Captura is a Series A climate & energy company founded by Harry Atwater and CX Xiang that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 private peers in the same sector and stage—Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—plus 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, anchoring to the $30M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yields $60M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy) to $1.8B (Watershed). The wide dispersion reflects material variation in business model maturity and market capture within the climate tech category.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $51M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $118M. The modest spread between stage-progression and peer-median reflects confidence in the comparable set, though the high end of peer range (Watershed at $1.8B) signals that outlier outcomes remain possible if Captura's technology or market position materially outperforms peers. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to Watershed and the path to revenue scale in carbon removal or related energy applications.
